summary: ChatGPT personal finance suite entered preview rollout via Plaid integration, escalating from announcement to shipping product for account connection and spend/planning queries.
Lumin Digital's Solaire AI-native intelligence layer received second-source confirmation with no scope expansion beyond prior coverage.
Fiserv agentOS agentic AI operating system for banking workflows received multiple second-source coverage cycles with no new product or partner facts beyond the May 14 launch.
Bud Financial's First Fidelity Bank deployment was second-sourced via Fintech Times with no new FI count or deal terms disclosed.
Plaid is building a proprietary transaction foundation model for categorization and insights, directly contesting One View's enrichment differentiation.
Plaid expanded its Perplexity integration to surface net worth, spend tracking, and AI-driven financial planning insights.
Plaid launched a new Income engine with finer taxonomy and configurable calculation controls, raising the feature bar in income verification.
Plaid expanded Bank Intelligence with Fraud Insights and a Primacy Score loyalty metric targeting FI-facing decisioning, overlapping Compass and One View.
JPMorgan signed executed fee agreements with multiple fintech aggregators, converting data-access costs from speculation to contracted reality and resetting Vault unit economics.
CFPB will not prioritize enforcement of its 2024 BNPL Reg Z digital-accounts rule, easing near-term compliance burden for BNPL-adjacent payment flows.
Plaid is now the default consumer-permissioned data rail for frontier AI platforms, combining data connectivity and intelligence layers for third-party AI surfaces.
OpenAI launched a Plaid-powered personal finance dashboard inside ChatGPT for Pro users on May 15, directly competing with Compass and One View.
Two additional Q2 Holdings 8-Ks (Items 5.02) extend leadership-churn pattern; cumulative four filings now on record.
OCC preempted Illinois's interchange fee law, federalizing payment-fee rulemaking and reshaping legal terrain for ASA Pay fee and rev-share structures.
Jack Henry 8-K (Item 5.02) discloses a second undisclosed director/officer change event; counterparty and role still not public.
Lumin Digital previewed expansion of its Compounding Growth Platform beyond digital banking, one day after its Solaire AI launch.
Fiserv launched agentOS, an agentic AI operating system for banking built with OpenAI and AWS, co-developed with six banks.
Former CFPB director Rohit Chopra named to lead a new consolidated California consumer and financial oversight agency combining DCA and DFPI.
Q2 Holdings filed two 8-Ks (2025-11-05 and 2025-02-12) disclosing undisclosed director/officer changes, suggesting a pattern of leadership churn.
Lumin Digital launched Solaire, a native AI intelligence layer in its growth platform, creating an in-channel alternative to Compass-style insights for Lumin-hosted FIs.
Jack Henry filed an 8-K (Item 5.02) on 2025-08-27 disclosing an undisclosed director or officer change at ASA's primary distribution channel.
FDX launched a formal initiative to define safe financial-data-sharing standards for agentic AI, the first standards-body action scoping agent access.
Jim Magats (ex-PayPal) was named MX CEO in March 2025; item resurfaced with no new development, over 12 months old.
Jack Henry filed an 8-K on 2026-03-26 disclosing entry into and termination of a material definitive agreement; counterparty and scope undisclosed.
First Fidelity Bank deployed Bud Financial per Feb–Mar 2026 republished reports; no new FI count or contract value beyond prior Banno/Bud coverage.
Third source confirms FIS-Anthropic agentic AI launch is scoped to financial crimes only, with no expansion into customer-facing or commercial intelligence yet.
Alacriti partnered with Socure to embed identity-based fraud prevention into instant payments rails, extending Socure beyond onboarding into transaction-time decisioning.
Business Wire second-sources Socure's dual appointment of Chung-Man Tam as CPO and Pablo Abreu as Chief AI and Innovation Officer, confirming an AI-native product reorg.
Socure named Chung-Man Tam as CPO and Pablo Abreu as Chief AI and Innovation Officer, signaling an AI-native identity decisioning acceleration.
Fifth Third's Newline extended its MCP server with a Skills feature, confirming institutional MCP adoption as a banking-platform pattern.
Fiserv reported revenue decline with confirmed core banking client losses, validating JANA activist thesis and creating FI migration opportunities.
CFPB directed $46M to Synapse/Evolve victims in a first-of-kind fintech middleware regulatory action establishing new middleware liability precedent.
Alloy launched perpetual KYB and continuous customer risk assessment, expanding from point-in-time onboarding into continuous monitoring adjacent to ASA Verify.
Q2 Holdings raised 2026 guidance and unveiled native AI fraud detection tools; shares rose 5.5%, signaling channel partners building fraud capabilities in-house.
New York proposed open banking-style consumer financial data access legislation, creating a state-level track distinct from the stalled federal CFPB Section 1033 pathway.
Fiserv acquired SCG to strengthen its core banking position amid ongoing activist investor pressure.
Plaid's March 2026 update adds AI document verification and an Encompass LOS Home Lending Report integration, expanding into Verify/Auth identity and lending-workflow territory.
CFPB Supervision Division issued a 'Humility Pledge' signaling procedural changes and a softer exam posture under new leadership, reducing compliance burden on FI partners.
